title: Is It Too Late to Take Our Country Back?
summary: The article analyzes Romania's democratic crisis at the start of 2026, focusing on the Constitutional Court (CCR) blockage over magistrates' special pensions, orchestrated by PSD-aligned judges and AUR lawyer Silvia Uscov. Author Rodica Culcer criticizes President Nicușor Dan's 'gradual' reform approach and warns that negotiations with PSD over intelligence service appointments could perpetuate the corrupt system rather than dismantle it.
talking_points: AUR lawyer Silvia Uscov filed legal challenges coordinated with PSD-controlled courts to block CCR from ruling on magistrates' special pensions law
The Bucharest Court of Appeal (CAB) has a pattern of delaying corruption cases until statute of limitations expires, including fugitive Sebastian Ghiță's cases
President Nicușor Dan's promise of 'gradual' change is criticized as insufficient given 36 years of institutional capture by corrupt networks
Negotiations with PSD over intelligence service directors (SRI, SIE) risk perpetuating the old securitate-linked establishment
The potential appointment of sovereignist lawyer Gabriel Zbârcea to lead SRI would signal no real reform of intelligence services
author: Rodica Culcer
